title: Electricity, Circuits and Ohm's Law
subject: Science
learningOutcomes: Understand Ohm's Law $V = IR$ connecting Voltage, Current, and Resistance.
Simulate electric circuits with battery, glowing light bulb, switch, and resistor.

objectives.text: Set $V=12$ V and $R=4$ Ω to measure electric current $I=3$ A.
Toggle switch OPEN/CLOSED to control electron flow animation.

theory: Ohm's Law states that electric current $I$ flowing through a conductor is directly proportional to potential difference $V$ across its ends ($V = IR$).
hints: Click the Switch button or toggle switch in the circuit workspace to open/close circuit.
Increase voltage to observe the light bulb glow brighter.

tasks.instruction: Toggle the switch status to CLOSED.

observationPrompts: What happens to current $I$ if resistance $R$ is doubled while voltage $V$ remains constant?

realWorldExamples.title: Household Circuit Breakers
realWorldExamples.description: Fuses and breakers protect appliances by limiting maximum current $I = V/R$.

commonMistakes: Confusing electric current (flow of charge in Amperes) with voltage (potential difference in Volts).
